name: Southwest Airlines

carryOn.notes: Southwest allows one carry-on bag and one personal item free for all passengers. No carry-on bag fees.

economy.notes: Two checked bags FREE for all passengers. This is a signature Southwest benefit.

business.notes: Included — same as economy, 2 bags free.

batteryRules.lithiumIon: Spare lithium-ion batteries and power banks allowed in carry-on only
batteryRules.spareBatteries: Must be carried on. Up to 100Wh no approval needed. 101–160Wh requires airline approval.
batteryRules.powerBankLimit: 100Wh unrestricted; up to 160Wh with airline approval
batteryRules.laptops: Allowed in carry-on. Remove from bag at TSA security checkpoint.

restrictedItems: Smart bags with non-removable lithium batteries over 160Wh

faqs.question: Does Southwest charge for checked bags?
faqs.answer: No. Southwest allows every passenger to check two bags free of charge, up to 50 lbs each. This applies to all fare types including Wanna Get Away.

faqs.question: What is Southwest's carry-on bag policy?
faqs.answer: Southwest allows one carry-on bag (up to 24 x 16 x 10 in) and one personal item per passenger at no charge. All passengers can use overhead bin space.

faqs.question: Can I bring a power bank on Southwest?
faqs.answer: Yes, power banks must be in your carry-on bag — not in checked luggage. Power banks up to 100Wh are permitted without special approval. Between 101–160Wh requires approval.
